reason: historical attempt import from vela-science/erdos-frontier@121807e20b06eb8148f594dfe13e440181252b0f:attack/attempt-ledger.v2.json (provenance, not a verdict)

attempt.claim: Erdos #124: computationally verified the Burr-Erdos-Graham-Li cofiniteness conjecture for ALL 176 admissible finite D with elements in [3,12] and sizes 3-5, at k=1. Every one is cofinite (Sigma(S(D,1)) contains all large n), with largest non-representable N(D) <= 986 (max at {3,4,6}; {3,4,7} reproduces BEGL's value 581, validating the method). This extends BEGL's single explicitly verified case {3,4,7} to 176 families and finds NO counterexample. Scope is honest and limited: k=1 only and bounded element range/size; the uniform statement over all admissible D and all k>=1 (the open core) is NOT established, and k>=2 verification requires larger search windows than used here (sparse D such as {3,6,9,10,12} push the threshold beyond a 3e5 window). This is broad verification, not the uniform proof.
attempt.detail: {"N_window":60000,"all_cofinite":true,"argmax":"{3,4,6}","begl_crosscheck":"{3,4,7} -> 581 (matches BEGL)","count":176,"element_range":"[3,12]","k":1,"margin":"986 << 60000 (reliable)","max_N":986,"open_core":"uniform equal-step subset-identity construction for all admissible finite D (att_cc286adad5113583)","provenance_note":"Opus direct computational attack; reproduces BEGL {3,4,7}=581.","scope_limits":["k=1 only","elements <=12, sizes 3-5","k>=2 needs larger windows (false-positive cofiniteness near cap)","uniform proof over all D,k is the open core, not established"],"sizes":"3-5","verified":"scripts/verify_124_database.py"}
